• Analysts point to capital expenditure by the government and healthy private consumption as key drivers of growth.
• This development bodes well for India's economic standing and investor confidence in the near term.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Definition of GDP and its components (consumption, investment, government spending, net exports).
Key economic indicators used to assess national economic health.
Role of international financial institutions like IMF, World Bank, ADB in economic forecasting.
Factors contributing to economic growth in India (e.g., domestic demand, manufacturing, services).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's economic resilience in the face of global headwinds and discuss its sustainability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government's fiscal and monetary policies in driving economic growth and managing inflation.
Discuss the challenges that India still faces in achieving inclusive and sustainable economic development despite positive GDP forecasts.
Examine the role of domestic demand and capital expenditure in India's growth trajectory.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant upward-trending bar graph representing economic growth, with a map of India in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to be the world's third-largest economy by 2027 (IMF), surpassing Japan and Germany.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Propulsion System for Future Deep-Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful static test of an advanced propulsion system.
• This next-generation engine is designed to power India’s future deep-space exploration missions, including potential lunar and Martian probes.
• The technology aims to significantly reduce travel time and increase payload capacity for interplanetary journeys.
• This achievement marks a crucial step in enhancing India's capabilities in space science and exploration.
• It positions ISRO at the forefront of developing innovative propulsion technologies for ambitious space endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's major missions (e.g., Chandrayaan, Mangalyaan, Gaganyaan).
Types of rocket propulsion systems (e.g., solid, liquid, cryogenic, electric).
Significance of advanced propulsion for deep-space missions.
Key objectives of India's space program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenously developed propulsion technologies for India's strategic autonomy in space.
Analyze how advancements in space propulsion can enable more ambitious deep-space missions and scientific discoveries.
Evaluate India's position in global space exploration and the potential socio-economic benefits of its space program.
Examine the challenges ISRO faces in developing cutting-edge space technologies and competing with global players.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a futuristic rocket engine firing, with stars and planets in the background, symbolizing deep-space travel.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the fourth entity to reach Mars and the first Asian nation to do so.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches New Initiative for Sustainable Urban Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a comprehensive new initiative focused on sustainable urban waste management.
• The program aims to promote circular economy principles, reduce landfill burden, and enhance waste-to-energy projects in cities nationwide.
• It emphasizes source segregation, recycling infrastructure development, and public awareness campaigns for responsible waste disposal.
• The initiative seeks to involve urban local bodies, private sector, and community groups in achieving its targets.
• This move is critical for addressing India's growing urban waste challenge and achieving environmental sustainability goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016: key provisions and responsibilities.
Concept of circular economy and its relevance to waste management.
Different methods of waste disposal (landfilling, incineration, composting, recycling).
Government initiatives like Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (Urban) and their focus on waste management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges in urban solid waste management in India and evaluate the effectiveness of current policies.
Analyze how the adoption of circular economy principles can transform urban waste management practices and contribute to sustainable development.
Suggest innovative technological and policy interventions for improving waste collection, processing, and disposal in Indian cities.
Examine the role of public participation and behavioral change in achieving effective waste management outcomes.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing different colored bins for waste segregation, leading to recycling and energy generation, symbolizing circular economy.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of solid waste every day, with a significant portion remaining untreated.

GS3.headline: India Conducts Major Joint Military Exercise with Key ASEAN Partner in Bay of Bengal
GS3.explainer:
• India today commenced a large-scale joint military exercise with a prominent ASEAN member state in the Bay of Bengal.
• The exercise, focusing on maritime security and interoperability, aims to enhance defense cooperation and address shared challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.
• It includes complex naval maneuvers, anti-submarine warfare drills, and humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) simulations.
• This collaboration underscores India's 'Act East' policy and its commitment to strengthening strategic ties with Southeast Asian nations.
• The exercise sends a strong signal regarding regional stability and collective security in a strategically vital maritime corridor.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Major joint military exercises of India (e.g., Malabar, Garuda, Sampriti).
ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations): its members and objectives.
India's 'Act East' Policy and its strategic importance.
Geostrategic significance of the Bay of Bengal and Indo-Pacific region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of joint military exercises with ASEAN partners for India's 'Act East' policy and regional security objectives.
Discuss how enhanced maritime cooperation contributes to maintaining a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in building defense partnerships with Southeast Asian nations amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
Examine the role of HADR operations in strengthening regional goodwill and cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: Naval ships from two different nations sailing in formation during a joint military exercise in open waters.
GS3.trivia: The Bay of Bengal is home to some of the world's busiest shipping lanes and is strategically crucial for global trade and energy security.

GS3.headline: RBI Issues Comprehensive Guidelines for FinTech Companies on Data Privacy and Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released a comprehensive set of guidelines for FinTech companies operating in India, primarily focusing on data privacy and security.
• The guidelines mandate robust data localization requirements, strict encryption protocols, and enhanced consent mechanisms for customer data usage.
• They aim to protect consumer interests, prevent data breaches, and ensure the stability of the rapidly growing FinTech sector.
• The framework also outlines responsibilities for data governance, auditing, and incident reporting for all regulated FinTech entities.
• This move signifies RBI's proactive approach to regulating the digital finance ecosystem and fostering trust among users.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Role and functions of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) as a regulator.
Concept of FinTech and its various segments (e.g., payments, lending, wealth management).
Importance of data privacy and cyber security in the digital age.
Key provisions of India's proposed Data Protection Bill (if enacted by this date, otherwise general data protection principles).
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing importance of FinTech in India's financial landscape and the associated risks and opportunities.
Analyze how RBI's new guidelines on data privacy and security can foster trust and ensure the sustainable growth of the FinTech sector.
Critically evaluate the challenges of balancing innovation with regulation in the rapidly evolving digital finance ecosystem.
Examine the implications of data localization mandates for global FinTech players operating in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of digital data flowing securely between a mobile phone and a cloud icon, with a lock symbol, representing data security in FinTech.
GS3.trivia: India has one of the fastest-growing FinTech markets globally, driven by initiatives like UPI and Jan Dhan accounts.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Infuses Capital into 200+ Startups in Latest Tranche
GS3.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) today announced its latest tranche of funding, supporting over 200 early-stage startups across various sectors.
• The scheme, a flagship initiative of the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), aims to provide financial assistance for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• This infusion of capital is crucial for nurturing innovation and entrepreneurship, especially in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• The supported startups span sectors like AgriTech, HealthTech, FinTech, and DeepTech, reflecting India's diverse startup ecosystem.
• SISFS plays a vital role in de-risking early-stage investments and promoting a robust startup culture in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Startup India initiative: objectives and key pillars.
Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS): purpose, eligibility, and funding mechanism.
Role of DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade).
Different stages of startup funding (seed, Series A, B, etc.).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of government-backed seed funding schemes like SISFS in fostering entrepreneurship and innovation in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how such schemes help mitigate risks.
Evaluate the role of startups in driving economic growth, creating employment, and promoting inclusive development.
Suggest further policy measures to strengthen India's startup ecosystem and enable global competitiveness.
GS3.imageDescription: A group of diverse young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern co-working space, symbolizing the vibrant startup culture.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, after the US and China.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists today announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The newly identified frog species, characterized by its unique vocalizations and distinct morphological features, highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region.
• This discovery underscores the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Researchers used a combination of morphological, genetic, and bioacoustic analyses to confirm the species as new to science.
• Such findings are crucial for understanding evolutionary patterns and developing targeted strategies for biodiversity protection.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats: designation as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Importance of amphibians as bio-indicators.
Factors contributing to biodiversity loss.
Conservation initiatives in India (e.g., Project Tiger, Project Elephant).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze how the discovery of new species reinforces the need for stronger biodiversity conservation policies in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies in protecting endemic and endangered species in India.
Examine the role of scientific research in identifying and conserving biodiversity.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a small, distinctly colored frog, camouflaged among green leaves in a tropical forest setting.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species, including amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals.
